---
title: "Python Setup & Viewing"
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Python Setup & Viewing}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r}
#| include: false
#| eval: false
library(freestiler)
```

## Overview

freestiler's Python package shares the same Rust engine as the R package. This
article covers Python-specific setup: installation, virtual environments,
building with optional features, and viewing tiles.

For usage examples (basic tiling, multi-layer, feature dropping, clustering,
coalescing, file input, SQL queries), see the
[Getting Started](getting-started.html) article, which covers R and Python
side-by-side.

## Prerequisites

You need a Rust toolchain installed. If you don't have one:

```bash
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
```

You also need Python 3.9+ and pip.

## Installation

### Virtual environment setup

```bash
cd python
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate   # On Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate
```

### Install from source

```bash
pip install -e .
```

This builds the Rust extension and installs the package in editable mode.
GeoPandas and its dependencies are installed automatically.

### Build with maturin

For development builds with maturin directly:

```bash
pip install maturin
python3 -m maturin develop --release
```

## Optional features

freestiler supports optional Rust features for direct file input and DuckDB
queries. These require additional Rust dependencies and are not enabled by
default.

### GeoParquet support

Read GeoParquet files directly in Rust without going through GeoPandas:

```bash
FREESTILER_GEOPARQUET=1 pip install -e .
```

### DuckDB support

Run SQL queries through DuckDB's spatial extension:

```bash
FREESTILER_DUCKDB=1 pip install -e .
```

### Both features

```bash
FREESTILER_GEOPARQUET=1 FREESTILER_DUCKDB=1 pip install -e .
```

## Viewing tiles

### py-maplibregl (MVT)

py-maplibregl bundles MapLibre GL JS 5.3, which supports MVT but not yet MLT.
Create tiles with `tile_format="mvt"`:

```python
import geopandas as gpd
from freestiler import freestile
from maplibre import Map, MapOptions
from maplibre.sources import VectorTileSource
from maplibre.layers import Layer, LayerType

gdf = gpd.read_file("nc.shp")
freestile(gdf, "nc_mvt.pmtiles", layer_name="counties", tile_format="mvt")

source = VectorTileSource(url="pmtiles://nc_mvt.pmtiles")

layer = Layer(
    type=LayerType.FILL,
    id="counties",
    source="counties-source",
    source_layer="counties",
    paint={"fill-color": "#00897b", "fill-opacity": 0.5}
)

m = Map(MapOptions(center=[-79.5, 35.5], zoom=6))
m.add_source("counties-source", source)
m.add_layer(layer)
m
```

### leafmap (MVT)

leafmap also currently requires MVT format:

```python
import leafmap.maplibregl as leafmap

m = leafmap.Map(center=[-79.5, 35.5], zoom=6)
m.add_pmtiles("nc_mvt.pmtiles", style={
    "layers": [{
        "id": "counties",
        "type": "fill",
        "source-layer": "counties",
        "paint": {"fill-color": "#00897b", "fill-opacity": 0.5}
    }]
})
m
```

### MapLibre GL JS in the browser (MLT or MVT)

For MLT support, use MapLibre GL JS 5.17+ directly. PMTiles files can be served
from any static file host:

```html
<script src="https://unpkg.com/pmtiles/dist/pmtiles.js"></script>
<script src="https://unpkg.com/maplibre-gl/dist/maplibre-gl.js"></script>
<script>
  let protocol = new pmtiles.Protocol();
  maplibregl.addProtocol("pmtiles", protocol.tile);

  new maplibregl.Map({
    container: "map",
    style: {
      version: 8,
      sources: {
        counties: {
          type: "vector",
          url: "pmtiles://nc_counties.pmtiles"
        }
      },
      layers: [{
        id: "county-fill",
        type: "fill",
        source: "counties",
        "source-layer": "counties",
        paint: { "fill-color": "#00897b", "fill-opacity": 0.5 }
      }]
    }
  });
</script>
```

## MLT viewer status

MLT is a new format (January 2026). Current viewer support:

| Viewer | MVT | MLT |
|--------|-----|-----|
| mapgl (R) | Yes | Yes |
| py-maplibregl | Yes | Not yet (bundles GL JS 5.3) |
| leafmap | Yes | Not yet |
| MapLibre GL JS 5.17+ | Yes | Yes |

As Python viewers upgrade their bundled MapLibre GL JS to 5.17+, MLT will work
everywhere. Until then, use `tile_format="mvt"` when creating tiles for Python
viewing.

## R vs Python API

The Python API mirrors R closely, with Pythonic conventions:

| Feature | R | Python |
|---------|---|--------|
| Input type | sf data frame | GeoDataFrame |
| Multi-layer | Named list of sf objects | Dict of GeoDataFrames |
| Default format | `"mlt"` | `"mlt"` |
| Zoom range | `min_zoom = 0, max_zoom = 14` | `min_zoom=0, max_zoom=14` |
| Feature dropping | `drop_rate = 2.5` | `drop_rate=2.5` |
| Base zoom | `base_zoom = 10` | `base_zoom=10` |
| Point clustering | `cluster_distance = 50` | `cluster_distance=50` |
| Feature coalescing | `coalesce = TRUE` | `coalesce=True` |
| Layer helper | `freestile_layer()` | (use dict directly) |
